setting.note: A single night on a Haarlem quay in the Hunger Winter of 1945 sets the whole book in motion: a collaborator shot in the dark, a family burned out and killed in reprisal, one boy left alive. The map holds Haarlem, where it happens, against the Amsterdam of the postwar decades, where the surviving child grows into a doctor and slowly learns what that night really was. The two cities sit about twenty kilometres apart across the North Holland flatland.

rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.

palette.note: The banked-fire red of a house burning on a frozen quay. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
